steps.title: Identify the grammar issue
steps.content: The subject is Maya Angelou (singular). The phrase "along with others such as Lorenzo Thomas and Gwendolyn Brooks" is a parenthetical modifier set off by commas — it does not change the subject's number. Strip it away: "The writer Maya Angelou ______ commonly associated with the Black Arts movement."

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A: have been — plural auxiliary "have." Maya Angelou is one person; plural verb disagrees. Eliminated.

B: is — singular linking verb. "Maya Angelou is commonly associated" — subject and verb agree. Correct.

C: are — plural linking verb. Same number error as A. Eliminated.

D: were — plural past. Two errors: plural number and past tense, while the passage describes an ongoing association. Eliminated.
